world.title: After unsuccessful US-Iran talks, what next for Trump?
world.summary: President Donald Trump confronts difficult decisions following unsuccessful US-Iran peace talks. A suggested blockade of the Strait of Hormuz may heighten tensions and raise global economic concerns. Analysts express skepticism about this approach, highlighting potential dangers to US military personnel and the possibility of voter discontent. Meanwhile, Iran's Revolutionary Guards issue a strong warning regarding the situation.

world.title: Nigerian airstrike hits market, 200 feared dead in Yobe state
world.summary: Nigerian military jets mistakenly struck a village market in Yobe state while pursuing Islamist militants on Saturday night, resulting in at least 200 feared dead. Local officials and residents reported the incident, highlighting the tragic consequences of the airstrike. The Nigerian Air Force has faced criticism for its operations in civilian areas amid ongoing conflicts with militant groups.

world.title: ‘Trumpian revolution’ cracking: Defeat imminent in Europe as Hungary votes, MAGA fractures at home too
world.summary: Hungary's recent election poses a significant challenge to Prime Minister Viktor Orban's 16-year rule and his concept of "illiberal democracy." This election is seen as a critical test for Orban, who has shifted from left-wing to far-right politics. Former President Trump has endorsed Orban's approach as a model for his Make America Great Again movement.

business.title: Trade Setup for April 13: Top 15 things to know before the opening bell as US-Iran talks end without agreement
business.summary: US-Iran talks concluded without an agreement, impacting market sentiment. The technical setup showed improvement, highlighted by a bullish crossover in momentum indicators. The index maintained its position above short-term moving averages, indicating potential for further gains. Analysts suggest that sustaining levels above 24,000 will be crucial for short-term market performance as traders prepare for the opening bell.

business.title: How are China and Iran cornering US without firing a shot amid tensions in Gulf
business.summary: China and Iran are leveraging global supply chains and strategic chokepoints to challenge the United States without direct military confrontation. This approach is reshaping economic warfare and highlighting vulnerabilities in U.S. strategies. A report by The Washington Post analyzes these developments, emphasizing how both nations are effectively countering U.S. influence in the Gulf region amid rising tensions.

business.title: Q4 results: ICICI Prudential AMC, Just Dial, Swaraj Engines among 7 firms to release earnings on April 13; check list
business.summary: Seven companies, including ICICI Prudential AMC, Just Dial, and Swaraj Engines, will announce their Q4 earnings for FY26 on April 13. ICICI Prudential AMC and Swaraj Engines are also expected to declare their final dividends for the fiscal year. Other firms reporting include Continental Controls and Ind Bank Housing, among others, contributing to the financial updates for the quarter.
